app.name: fzf-make
app.one_liner: A command line tool that executes commands using fuzzy finder with preview window for make, npm, pnpm, yarn, just and task.

screens.name: Command Selection Screen
screens.purpose: Display list of available commands from detected task runners with fuzzy search and live preview of command details.

rust_commands.name: copy_command_to_clipboard
rust_commands.purpose: Copy the currently selected command string to the system clipboard using Ctrl+Y keybinding.

rust_commands.name: execute_selected_command
rust_commands.purpose: Execute the user-selected command from the fuzzy finder interface, optionally with additional arguments provided via popup.

rust_commands.name: repeat_last_command
rust_commands.purpose: Re-execute the most recently run command when invoked with `--repeat` flag.

mvp_plan: week 1: Add preview window showing command definition
week 1: Implement core fuzzy selection TUI with Ratatui
week 1: Integrate parsing for make and npm scripts
week 1: Support basic execution without arguments
acceptance_tests: npm workspace scripts are collected and displayed when running in an npm workspace (v8.16+).
Tool correctly handles complex Makefile constructs like $(eval $(shell ...)) without hanging.
User can copy selected npm script command to clipboard using Ctrl+Y.
User can select and execute a make target from a Makefile using fuzzy search with preview.
